rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LEUNG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 12, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG's (Amended) Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate. The hearing on petition for writ of mandate is off calendar. On May 8, 2014, and June 23, 2014, the court ordered petitioner to provide courtesy copies of his papers and properly tabbed exhibits in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B and CRC 1110 (f) not less than two weeks prior to the new hearing dates. On August 11, 2014, the Court received courtesy copies of the Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandate and the Memorandum of Points and Authorities, and a letter from Petitioner stating that Petitioner would be submitting a new copy of the administrative record with index tabs and a more detailed index "hopefully by the close of business" on August 11, 2014. These documents were untimely given the two prior orders continuing the hearing. Petitioner must re-notice a hearing on the Petition in order to obtain a new hearing date, and must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers and properly tabbed exhibits in compliance with Local Rule 2.6B and CRC 1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway Inc.S Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 12, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway Inc.'s Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Defendant Safeway may appear via court call. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JANE VILTMAN VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Pltf And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 12, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITAL, DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Pltf And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion is granted. All objections are overruled. Plaintiff to serve verified responses without any objections and produce documents no later than August 29, 2014. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted against Plaintiff Jane Viltman in the amount of $1480 and to be made payable to Sutter West Bay Hospitals and attorneys Davidovitz & Bennett no later than August 29, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to kgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IVAN RODRIGUEZ VS. VEOLIA TRANSPORTATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One, Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 12, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF IVAN RODRIGUEZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One, Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions. The motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: (1) The motion is denied as to form interrogatory numbers 7.1, 7.2, and 7.3. The form interrogatories call for the responding party to state if responding party is making any claim for property damage. 7.1(a) is a "yes or no" question, and the defendants responded "no." As a result, the subparts of 7.1, and the supplemental questions in 7.2 and 7.3 are not applicable. (2) The motion is granted as to form interrogatories 13.1 and 13.2. (a) The question must be answered if anyone other than the litigation attorneys conducted or ordered surveillance of the plaintiff. Only the litigation attorneys are entitled to the privilege. (b) As to surveillance conducted at the instigation of litigation defense counsel, a qualified work product privilege might apply under Coito v. Superior Court (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, but defendant has not met its burden to establish the privilege. The Court does not find that the disclosure of whether there were surveillance videos alone qualifies for an absolute work product privilege. (3) The motion with respect to Requests for Production numbers 10 and 11 is granted in part and denied in part as follows: (a) The defendants must produce any or all photographs or video footage obtained or produced by the parties themselves, their employees, agents or contractors. (b) The motion is denied without prejudice as to any photographs or video footage produced by or at the specific direction of defendant's litigation counsel. Should subsequent answers to form interrogatory numbers 13.1 and 13.2 disclose the existence of unprivileged surveillance photographs or videos, the motion to compel production could be renewed. (4) The request for sanctions is denied. The objections were substantially justified. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CARMEN DIAZ ECHAURI VS. EKO VENTURE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Subpoenas And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 12, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CARMEN ECHAURI's Motion To Quash Subpoenas And For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to quash is denied. Personnel records come within the zone of privacy protected by Article I, §1 of the California Constitution. See Board of Trustees v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA3d 516. The privilege is not absolute. When an individual's right of privacy in his personal affairs conflicts with the public need for discovery in litigation, the competing interests must be carefully balanced. See Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 C.3d 391. Defendant has made a sufficient showing that the discovery sought regarding plaintiff's past job performance in these prior employment records is directly relevant to the subject matter of the litigation and essential to the resolution of this lawsuit, such that defendant's right to this discovery outweighs plaintiff's privacy rights in the information sought. The subpoenaed documents are to be produced under a protective order for "attorney's eyes only." Requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHAD FOLKERS VS. SAFEWAY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Privilege Log; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 12, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC. Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Privilege Log; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion is granted and denied as follows: Document Requests # 1, 2, 12, 16: Denied. The request is not reasonably particularized as required by CCP 2031.030(c). Document Requests #3, 5, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 17, 18, 19: Granted. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. Document Requests #4: Granted. Plaintiff's objection on the grounds of privacy is granted in part. Plaintiff may redact the last 4 numbers of his Social Security number See CC Section 1798.89. Document Requests #6: Granted. Objections are overruled. Nacht Lewis has been overruled by Coito v. Superior Court (2012) 54 Cal 4th 480. If plaintiff is claiming a document is protected by the work product privilege then they must list it in a privilege log. Document Request #7, 8: Granted. Objections are overruled. Witnesses are discoverable. See CCP Section 2017.010. If plaintiff is claiming a document is protected by the work product privilege then they must list it in a privilege log. Plaintiff to serve verified responses and produce documents to Document Requests 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 17, 18 and 19 no later than August 29, 2014. Response to be in compliance with CCP Section 2031.220-240 which includes a privilege log for any document withheld on the claim of attorney work product. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted against Plaintiff Chad Folkers in the amount of $1360 to be made payable to Safeway Inc. and their attorneys of record Vogl Meredith Burke by August 29, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to kgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA VS. MEDIVATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Medivation, Inc. And Medivation Prostate Therapeutics, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 12, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANTS MEDIVATION, INC., and MEDIVATION PROSTATE THERAPEUTICS, INC.'s Motion To Stay. Denied. Defendants have not made a sufficient showing that the appeal of the Statement of Decision in Medivation et al. v. The Regents of the University of California, et al. (SF Superior Court case no. CGC-11-510715) ("Medivation I") warrants staying this distinct action. Although both cases involve the same contract and the same parties, Defendants have not established that the outcome of the appeal will substantially affect this case. The Statement of Decision in Medivation I considers the narrow issue of whether sales milestone payments are "Sublicensing Income," as defined in the Exclusive License Agreement ("ELA"). The Statement of Decision did not broadly define "Sublicensing Income." Because the narrow issue on appeal is whether sales milestone payments are "Sublicensing Income," the question presented in this case, whether profit-sharing revenue should be considered "Sublicensing Income" under the ELA, should be unaffected by the outcome of the appeal. A stay is not necessary to ensure consistency between legal rulings in the two cases. Further, a stay will not promote judicial economy. The parties can work together to avoid duplicative discovery from Medivation I, to the extent overlap exists. The Court does not address Medivation's arguments for stay based upon breach of fiduciary duty claims in the current case that have now been dismissed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
